assignment_description:
⍞ Materials: The Art of Asking Better Questions, Journal or Notebook, Bible

➜ READ
Intro-Ch.1, p.3-25

➜ REFLECT
Think about the individuals that you interact with on a daily basis. Identify someone that askes good questions and ask that person what motivstes him or her to ask good questions..

⍞ Materials: The Art of Asking Better Questions, Journal or Notebook, Bible

➜ READ
Ch. 2, p.26-39

➜ REVIEW & LIST
Review the italicized questions throughout this chapter and list three questions in your journal or notebookthat were intriguing to you. Explain why.

⍞ Materials: The Art of Asking Better Questions, Journal or Notebook, Bible

➜ READ
Ch. 3, p.40-57

➜ LISTEN & Track
Divide a piece of paper into four squares. Write one of the four levels of questions in each square. One day this week, listen for the four levels of questions being asked by those around you and track how many times you hear the four levels of questions throughout the day.

⍞ Materials: The Art of Asking Better Questions, Journal or Notebook, Bible

➜ READ
Ch. 4, p.61-71

➜ INTERVIEW
Identify one adult and one person your age to interview using David Brooks' questions listed on p.69. Give yourself plenty of time to interview each person. The goal is to get to know the person better, not simply to get through the questions.

⍞ Materials: The Art of Asking Better Questions, Journal or Notebook, Bible

➜ READ
Ch.5-6, p.72-92

➜ WRITE
In your own words, list and explain the six reasons Jesus asked questions.

After you read ch.5-6, Review Resource 4: Fascinating Facts About The Questions Jesus Asked on p.181-185.

⍞ Materials: The Art of Asking Better Questions, Journal or Notebook, Bible

➜ READ
Ch.7, p.93-101

➜ REFLECT & ASK
Reflect on the questions you have regarding God, the Bible, and faith. Ask the members of your family what questions they have regarding God, the Bible, and faith. Identify the questions that you have in common and as a family, take steps towards answering those questions.

⍞ Materials: The Art of Asking Better Questions, Journal or Notebook, Bible

➜ READ
Ch.8-9, p.105-123

➜ INTERVIEW
Identify a person who is responsible for leading people and schedule a time to interview that person. Identify questions from ch. 8-9 (especially from p, 111 & 116) to ask during the interview. Reflect on your own leadership influence and ask yourself the questions on p. 111 & 116. If time permits, interview several leaders and compare their responses.

⍞ Materials: The Art of Asking Better Questions, Journal or Notebook, Bible

➜ READ
Ch.10, p.126-137

➜ PLAN & IMPLEMENT
Designate a journal to be your Questions Journal. Reflect on the nine practices from ch. 9 and organize the journal accordingly. Do not be tempted to rush this assignment. The more time you spend creating a Questions Journal, the great likelihood you will see a transformation in how you ask questions.

⍞ Materials: The Art of Asking Better Questions, Journal or Notebook, Bible

➜ READ
Ch.11, p. 138-150

➜ PRACTICE
Identify a family member or friend to participate in a question-asking exercise with you. On p.147, read #6 "Hold a questions-only conversation with others." Once you both understand the exercise, initiate the conversation and see how long the discussion lasts. Keep practicing until the exercise becomes more natural.

⍞ Materials: The Art of Asking Better Questions, Journal or Notebook, Bible

➜ READ
Ch.12-Conclusion, p.151-160

➜ RECEIVE FEEDBACK
Identify two people that you interact with consistently and ask them to provide feedback on how you ask questions. Use the questions on p.152-153 to guide the conversation. The purpose of this exercise is to develop the skill of asking better questions. Do not be offended by the feedback you recieve. Embrace the feedback and develop action steps that will help you further develop the ability to ask great questions.

⍞ Materials: The Art of Asking Better Questions, Journal or Notebook, Bible

➜ READ
Resource 2: Questions To Ask Ourselves, p.171-173

➜ REFLECT & REVIEW
Review the Self-Reflection Questions on p.171-173. Which questions are the most difficult to answer? What changes do you want to make in light of these questions.

⍞ Materials: The Art of Asking Better Questions

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to course.
assignment_teachersNote:
★ Econ/Civics: Grade 11 (Canada) — TERM 1 EXAM QUESTIONS
· Explain how curiosity aids in developing the skill of asking great questions.

· If a friend asked you, "Why is it important to develop the skill of asking better questions?" how would you reply?
